content.subtitle: Structure
items: if/when/whenever + present simple, present simple

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Facts, routines, and outcomes that are generally true
Scientific or technical cause-and-effect statements

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If people consume too much sugar, their energy levels rise and then drop quickly.
When metal gets hot enough, it expands.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + present form, will + bare infinitive / imperative

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Real and possible future conditions with likely results
Conditional instructions and warnings

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If local authorities invest in public transport, traffic levels will fall over time.
If Maya calls before noon, tell her I'll join the meeting online.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Modal verbs and semi-modals can replace will in the result clause when meaning changes (ability, possibility, advice or obligation).
examples: If households reduce waste this month, we might avoid water restrictions in summer.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past simple / past continuous, would + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Unreal, unlikely or imaginary present/future situations
Hypothetical advice and speculation

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If the city had a larger cycling network, fewer commuters would drive to work.
What would you do if your internet connection failed during an online exam?

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Could and might often replace would to show ability or weaker certainty.
examples: If the weather improved, we could hold the event outdoors.

content.subtitle: Grammar Note
content.description: After if, both was and were are found with I/he/she/it in some varieties, but were is more formal and standard in fixed phrases.
examples: If I were in your position, I'd request a deadline extension.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past perfect, would have + past participle

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Imagined past conditions and imagined past results
Regret, criticism, and retrospective analysis

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If the team had tested the update earlier, they would have caught the bug before release.
The conference would have started on time if the keynote speaker had arrived earlier.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals can appear in the result clause to adjust meaning (certainty, possibility, criticism).
examples: If you had checked the contract carefully, you might have noticed the hidden fee.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: Past condition → present result: if + past perfect, would + bare infinitive
Present condition → past result: if + past simple, would have + past participle

content.subtitle: Uses
items: To connect different time frames in one hypothetical relationship

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If Leo had accepted the job abroad, he would live in Berlin now.
If I were less cautious, I would have invested in that project last year.

content.subtitle: Structures
items: unless + affirmative clause (meaning: if ... not)
as long as / so long as + clause
provided (that) / providing (that) + clause
suppose / supposing + clause
in case + clause / in case of + noun

content.subtitle: Uses
items: To express conditions with different levels of formality, emphasis and meaning

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We can postpone the launch as long as the client agrees to the new timeline.
Provided that all documents are signed, funding will be released tomorrow.
Supposing the data is incomplete, how would you verify the conclusion?
Take a charger in case your battery runs out during the trip.
Unless demand increases, the factory will reduce output next quarter.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Do not combine unless with another negative in the same clause.
In case means as a precaution; it does not mean if in all contexts.

content.subtitle: Structures
items: I wish / If only + past simple (present regret)
I wish / If only + past continuous (annoying ongoing situation)
I wish / If only + past perfect (past regret)
I wish + would (complaints about another person/situation)
It's (high/about) time + past simple
I'd rather + subject + past simple

content.subtitle: Uses
items: To express unreal desires, frustration and regret
To suggest that change is necessary now

content.subtitle: Examples
items: I wish we had more time to review the proposal.
If only they had informed us before publishing the update.
It's high time the committee made a final decision.
I'd rather you didn't share the draft yet.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Use hope, not wish, for realistic future expectations.
With I'd rather + subject, use a past form for present/future preference.

content.subtitle: Structures
items: Should + subject + base verb (instead of first conditional if-clause)
Were + subject + to + base verb (instead of second conditional if-clause)
Had + subject + past participle (instead of third conditional if-clause)

content.subtitle: Uses
items: To sound formal, concise, or literary in written English

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Should you need further assistance, contact the support desk.
Were the policy to change, many contracts would need revision.
Had the warning been issued earlier, the evacuation would have been smoother.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Do not use if together with inverted forms.
Negative inversion places not after the subject (e.g. Had we not acted...).

sections.title: A: Match to Make Sentences
sections.instructions: Match the sentence halves (1–8) with the correct endings (a–h) to form complete conditional sentences.

items.sentence: If you heat water to 100°C, **E** it boils.
She would have got the job **A** if she had prepared better for the interview.
Were the company to go bankrupt, **G** hundreds of employees would lose their jobs.
Unless you submit the form by Friday, **C** your application will not be considered.
If I had more free time, **F** I would take up a new hobby.
Provided that you follow the instructions carefully, **H** the experiment will yield accurate results.
Had the scientists discovered the vaccine sooner, **B** thousands of lives might have been saved.
Should you require any further assistance, **D** please do not hesitate to contact our team.

sections.title: B: Rewrite Using Conditionals
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence using the conditional structure indicated in brackets. Do not change the meaning.

items.sentence:
He didn't study hard enough, so he didn't pass the exam. (Third Conditional)
He ………………… the exam if he ………………… harder.

She isn't a native speaker, so she makes occasional errors. (Second Conditional)
If she ………………… a native speaker, she ………………… occasional errors.
items.answer: If she were a native speaker, she wouldn't make occasional errors.

items.sentence:
We will only sign the contract if they meet our conditions. (Provided that)
………………… they meet our conditions, we will sign the contract.
items.answer: Provided that they meet our conditions, we will sign the contract.

items.sentence:
I regret that I didn't take the opportunity when it arose. (If only)
………………… I had taken the opportunity when it arose.
items.answer: If only I had taken the opportunity when it arose.

items.sentence:
He didn't know about the delay, so he arrived at the usual time. (Mixed Conditional)
If he ………………… about the delay, he ………………… earlier.
items.answer: If he had known about the delay, he would have arrived earlier.

items.sentence:
They won't release the software unless they fix all the bugs. (Inverted Conditional)
………………… all the bugs fixed, they will not release the software.
items.answer: Should all the bugs be fixed, they will release the software. / Unless all the bugs are fixed, they will not release the software.

items.sentence:
The manager was absent, so no decisions were made. (Third Conditional)
If the manager ………………… present, decisions ………………… made.
items.answer: If the manager had been present, decisions would have been made.

items.sentence:
I don't have enough money, so I can't invest in property. (Second Conditional)
If I ………………… enough money, I ………………… in property.
items.answer: If I had enough money, I could invest in property.

sections.title: C: Complete the Text
sections.instructions: Read the text below and complete each gap with ONE suitable word.

items.sentence: According to research, (1) ...... a person develops strong coping mechanisms early in life, they are far more likely to handle adversity effectively as an adult.
Of course, this is easier said than done — (2) ...... we could simply teach resilience in schools the way we teach mathematics, the world would be a very different place.
But (3) ...... that is not yet fully possible, we must rely on a combination of therapy, mentorship and lived experience.
Consider what (4) ...... happen if a child were raised in an environment of constant criticism and instability.
Even (5) ...... that child later found supportive relationships in adulthood, the psychological scars might remain.
(6) ...... those scars never fully heal, the individual may still lead a fulfilling life — provided, that is, that they receive adequate support.
Experts suggest that (7) ...... it not for the social safety nets in many countries, the mental health crisis would be far worse.
(8) ...... we give this issue the attention it deserves, future generations will undoubtedly thank us.

sections.title: D: Circle the Correct Word or Phrase
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase in each sentence.

items.sentence: **If/Unless** you book in advance, you are unlikely to get a seat.
If only I **knew/know** the answer — I've been trying to work it out for hours.
**Were/Was** the project to be cancelled, we would lose a significant amount of funding.
She would have passed the test **if/unless** she had revised more carefully.
**Suppose/Supposing** we missed the deadline — what would the consequences be?
I'd rather you **didn't/don't** mention this to anyone else at the moment.
He will accept the offer, **as long as/even if** the salary meets his expectations.
**Had/Have** she been more careful, the accident could have been avoided entirely.
It's high time the government **took/takes** action on climate change.
We'll proceed with the plan **provided that/even if** everyone gives their approval.

sections.title: E: Complete with the Words in the Box
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with a word or phrase from the box. Use each item once only.

items.sentence: ………………… you encounter any problems during installation, please restart the device.
………………… the negotiations fail, the two countries may resort to legal arbitration.
She agreed to participate ………………… that her anonymity would be protected.
………………… I had paid more attention in class, I would understand this concept now.
………………… he known about the risk, he would have taken a different approach.
The exhibition will go ahead ………………… there are no further restrictions.
………………… they reduce the price, we simply cannot afford to buy it.
………………… you lose your passport abroad, you should contact the nearest embassy immediately.
They will fund the project ………………… that the targets are clearly defined.
………………… we had known about the cancellation, we would still have come.

sections.title: F: Write One Word in Each Gap
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence by writing ONE suitable word in the gap.

items.sentence: If the test results ………………… to show any abnormalities, the patient will need further assessment.
………………… she not been so exhausted, she would have stayed until the end of the meeting.
I wish I ………………… speak three languages fluently — it would open so many doors.
It's about time companies ………………… more responsibility for their environmental impact.
………………… you have any objections to the proposal, now is the time to raise them.
She acts as ………………… she has never made a mistake in her life.
The deal will go through, ………………… as both parties stick to the agreed terms.
………………… I known how complex the project was, I would have asked for more time.

sections.title: G: Rewrite the Sentences
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence using an inverted conditional structure, without changing the meaning.

items.sentence:
If you should need any help with your application, contact our support team.
Contact our support team ………………… .
items.answer: Should you need any help with your application, contact our support team.

items.sentence:
If she had not interrupted the speaker, the lecture would have continued smoothly.
………………… the lecture would have continued smoothly.
items.answer: Had she not interrupted the speaker, the lecture would have continued smoothly.

items.sentence:
If it were not for the generous funding, the research centre would close.
………………… for the generous funding, the research centre would close.
items.answer: Were it not for the generous funding, the research centre would close.

items.sentence:
If the government had taken action sooner, the crisis would have been avoided.
………………… the crisis would have been avoided.
items.answer: Had the government taken action sooner, the crisis would have been avoided.

items.sentence:
If there are any technical difficulties, the event will be postponed.
………………… any technical difficulties, the event will be postponed.
items.answer: Should there be any technical difficulties, the event will be postponed.

items.sentence:
If the economy were stronger, unemployment would fall significantly.
………………… unemployment would fall significantly.
items.answer: Were the economy stronger, unemployment would fall significantly.

sections.title: H: Choose the Correct Answer
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C or D) to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: ………………… the traffic not been so heavy, we would have arrived on time.
She would rather the meeting ………………… postponed until next week.
………………… you find the time, it would be worth reading the full report.
If the vaccine ………………… developed earlier, the pandemic would have been far less severe.
It's high time we ………………… a decision about the future of the company.
………………… the cost is reasonable, I'm willing to consider the proposal.
If I ………………… you, I would reconsider the offer before turning it down.
………………… she had listened to the advice, she might not have made such a costly error.

sections.title: I: Renewable Energy Cloze Text
sections.instructions: Read the text and write ONE suitable word in each numbered gap.

items.sentence: ...... governments had invested more heavily in solar and wind technology two decades ago, the energy crisis of recent years might have been significantly less severe.
...... nations commit to phasing out fossil fuels within the next two decades, scientists believe that global temperature rises can be kept to manageable levels.
...... the infrastructure for renewable energy not yet been fully developed in many regions, the challenge remains considerable.
...... countries work together and share technology, the pace of change could increase dramatically.
Critics argue that ...... the cost of renewable energy continues to fall, public and private investment will follow naturally.
...... it not for the technological advances of the past decade, many of these solutions would still be economically unviable.
Today, ...... as countries adopt consistent policy frameworks, the transition becomes increasingly feasible.
...... the political will exists, there is every reason to believe that a cleaner, more sustainable energy future is achievable within a generation.

sections.title: J: Keyword Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given in capitals. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
She didn't leave early, so she missed the last train.
**HAD**
She ………………… the last train.
items.answer: had left early, she wouldn't have missed

items.sentence:
I regret not applying for that position when I had the chance.
**ONLY**
If ………………… for that position when I had the chance.

It is unlikely that the proposal will be accepted without further revisions.
**UNLESS**
The proposal ………………… further revisions are made.

The project can go ahead on the condition that the budget is approved.
**PROVIDED**
The project ………………… the budget is approved.

It would be a good idea for you to speak to a financial advisor.
**WERE**
………………… you, I would speak to a financial advisor.

They did not notify us of the change, so we were unprepared.
**NOTIFIED**
Had ………………… of the change, we would have been prepared.

The children are allowed to stay up late only if they finish their homework first.
**LONG**
The children are allowed to stay up late ………………… they finish their homework first.

It was only because of her quick thinking that the situation was resolved.
**NOT**
Had it ………………… her quick thinking, the situation would not have been resolved.
